pi_items.source: Verified via setup_task.sh and export_result.sh scripts which explicitly use and export this file path.
Verified via setup_task.sh and export_result.sh scripts which explicitly expect the results in this file.
Verified via setup_sumo.sh which copies real-world Bologna scenario data to this directory inside the environment.
Verified by reading verifier.py (which asserts 'len(rows) >= 600') and pasubio.rou.xml (which contains departures until step 3598).

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating a Python TraCI script at /home/ga/SUMO_Output/simulate_breakdown.py to simulate a vehicle breakdown in the real-world derived Bologna Pasubio scenario. The script relies on target_edges.json to dynamically locate the critical and upstream edges. The vehicle must be stopped exactly at step 150 for 300 steps. The simulation must log the number of halted vehicles to /home/ga/SUMO_Output/breakdown_queue.csv for a minimum of 600 simulation steps. The verifier validates the Python logic (AST parsing for setSpeed and getLastStepHaltingNumber) and checks the CSV to ensure a localized traffic shockwave occurs (an increase in halted vehicles).
